div1.entryFree:
Probable, 1) likely, having more evidence than the contrary: As III, 5, 11. All's III, 6, 107. Oth. I, 2, 76. Ant. V, 2, 356. Cymb. II, 4, 115. IV, 2, 141.
2) calculated to bias the judgment, satisfactory: single I'll resolve you, which to you shall seem p., of every these happened accidents, Tp. V, 249. strenghtened with what apology you think may make it p. need, All's II, 4, 52. the least of all these signs were p. H6B III, 2, 178. it is spoke freely out of many mouths, -- how p. I do not know, Cor. IV, 6, 65.*
